level_signals: 1-3 years in a high growth technical environment
Comp range $185K-$235K base + equity, consistent with early-career IC at a well-funded AI startup
Role is intentionally broad and execution-oriented rather than managerial

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 1-3 years in a high growth technical environment. Roles that are inherently self-starters, entrepreneurial, or technical are also great fits, looking to make the switch into this new and emerging critical function for all companies.

some experience in some form with AI enablement, AI operations, or AI engineering roles. You are generally comfortable prompting your way to solutions, and sometimes needing to get into code / working with APIs, etc.

you are a curious learner and builder, skilled with new AI tools like Claude Code, Cursor, Notion AI, automation tools like Dust, Gumloop, n8n, Zapier, GTM enrichments like Clay and Unify, and search APIs like Exa, Parallel, Browserbase.
a bias toward action and building "v1 MVP" today. You thrive in the ambiguity of a fast-paced AI startup.

you can produce clear, concise presentation of information, and influence teams to use your products. You run training sessions that drive impact through leadership and communications, in every gap you find.
able to work hands-on in workflows across Sales, Post-Sales, Finance, People/HR, Ops, BizOps, Marketing, EPD, and more. It's OK if you haven't done it before.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Some familiarity with SQL, BI, managing data lakehouses, and scraping and scripting from DS notebooks, is a plus.

scope.surface: AI automation, internal tooling, analytics, workflow design, and AI culture across the entire company
primary_metrics_inferred: Additional work delivered via efficiency gains
Internal stakeholder satisfaction with AI tools
AI adoption / 'aha moment' count across teammates
core_responsibilities: Build AI automation agents to make the company efficient: design and deploy agents for automated workflows, custom internal tools, and programmatic systems
Help automate manual sales processes, embed AI into growth marketing engines, maximize Claude skills in the post-sales org
Build out an account mapping platform that helps sales and marketing identify the best individuals to target
Leverage scrapers, search APIs, workflow automation tools, and LLMs to build account mapping platform effectively and efficiently
Build an AI-native culture for the entire business by identifying and enabling internal 'AI champions' and builders
Define templates and best practices into team norms
Build out AI analytics: measure hours saved and additional work delivered as a result of efficiency and effectiveness gains

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($185K-$235K base + equity) for 1-3 year experience level
Direct embedding with Founders / leadership provides high visibility and learning
Clear articulation of core responsibilities and example workflows
Well-funded company (a16z, Accel, Bain Capital Ventures, Coatue, Index Ventures)
Explicitly states cross-functional experience is OK to not have previously ('It's OK if you haven't done it before')
Role explicitly acknowledges it is intentionally broad, setting clear expectations
red_flags: Fully in-office 5 days a week in San Francisco — eliminates all remote or hybrid candidates
Role is very broad and ambiguous ('intentionally broad') which may result in unclear success metrics or shifting priorities
High buzzword density and expansive scope may indicate role definition is still evolving
No explicit mention of visa sponsorship or work authorization

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years range 1-3 and comp range)
scope.team_size_estimate (described as 'small team' but number not stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and customer examples)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed as KPIs)
ambiguities: The role title is 'Founder's Office, Applied AI' which does not map cleanly to a single archetype — BizOps/Strategy & Ops was selected but it has strong Technical Program Manager and AI Ops flavors as well.

The JD says 'Roles that are inherently self-starters, entrepreneurial, or technical are also great fits, looking to make the switch' — it is unclear whether 1-3 years is a strict requirement or a guideline for career-changers.
Builder DNA requirement lists many tools as examples; it is unclear which are hard requirements vs. illustrative examples of the type of tools expected.
